per_ac.ac: file-path/omitted Impacted-Domains resolves to owning domain, ZERO adr-divergence-unowned
per_ac.note: Reuses existing fully-hermetic machinery in divergence_test.go (writeSpecAndPlan + writeADR + writeManifest + executor.MockExecutor). The RED is real: divergence.go L142 sets candidateDomains=meta.Domains, and contextpack/spec.go stores a file-path bullet (e.g. 'genevieve/review.py') as a bare domain NAME, so LoadOwnership looks under .mindspec/docs/domains/genevieve/review.py/OWNERSHIP.yaml and finds nothing -> unowned before the fix; resolves after. No LLM/network/git/harness.

per_ac.ac: named-domain path unchanged (no new false positives)
per_ac.note: Regression/guard test, mirrors existing TestValidateDivergenceCoveredDomainPasses. Passes both before and after the fix by design (locks no-regression). Legitimate guard, not a RED pin and not vacuous.
Mirrors existing TestUnownedFileRejected. Guards against the fix over-globbing the gate open (would RED if the fix accidentally disabled the gate). Hermetic, non-vacuous.

Pure message-text assertion on checkADRCoverage/ValidatePlan (plan.go L529). Current text is only 'run: mindspec adr create --domain X' -> RED before fix. Unit-testable without lifecycle; existing plan_test.go fixtures (TestPlanRejectsUncoveredDomain) are the template.

per_ac.ac: no ADR cited -> create remedy still surfaced
per_ac.note: Guard that the fix preserves the 'adr create' path. Hermetic. Non-vacuous as long as the fix changes the L529 message (AC1 forces that). Author should pick the right branch (empty-citations still routes through checkADRCoverage L529 for uncovered domains; the L143 adr-citations error is a separate diagnostic).

per_ac.ac: adr show/list find a worktree-local ADR (pre-fix FindRoot would miss it)
per_ac.note: Fully hermetic via existing cmd/mindspec/adr_test.go helpers setupWorktreePair + chdir, which fabricate the .git/worktrees/commondir linkage on disk (NO real git). Proven pattern: TestADRCreate_WritesIntoInvokingWorktree already invokes adrCreateCmd.RunE directly; the R3 test invokes adrShowCmd/adrListCmd.RunE the same way. RED is real: today's adr.go L97/L138 call FindRoot, which resolveWorktreeRoot's back to main where the ADR is absent -> store.Get errors; fix switches to FindLocalRoot/overlay. CAVEAT: AC says 'root-resolution helper' but no helper is extracted today; invoking RunE (or testing an extracted helper if the fix adds one) both satisfy it. Over-specification, not blocking.

Pure parser unit test in internal/adr/parse_test.go (existing tests all use the LIST form '- **Domain(s)**:'; parse.go matches via strings.Contains so the non-list form is genuinely new coverage). Explicitly a locking guard (parser unchanged per Scope) -> not a RED pin, and the spec is honest about that. Not vacuous: it pins behavior the spec promises to keep.

per_ac.ac: scaffoldPlan(specID) output contains literal adr_citations in frontmatter
per_ac.note: Trivially hermetic (pure string fn internal/approve/spec.go scaffoldPlan). Confirmed current scaffold emits only status/spec_id/version, NO adr_citations -> RED before fix.

Message-text assertion on ValidatePlan (plan.go L141/L143). Confirmed current text 'no ADR citations in frontmatter...' lacks the literal 'adr_citations' -> RED before fix. Two empty-citations branches exist (L141 WARN when fitness present, L143 ERROR when absent); test must target one. Existing TestValidatePlan_EmptyCitations_* are the template. Hermetic.

summary: All 9 ACs across R1-R4 are pinnable by hermetic, CI-runnable Go unit tests with NO LLM agent, network, real git, or slow harness scenario. The fixture machinery already exists and is directly reusable: R1 uses divergence_test.go's writeSpecAndPlan/writeADR/writeManifest + executor.MockExecutor (the exact divergence-gate scaffold); R2/R4 are message-text assertions on checkADRCoverage/ValidatePlan/scaffoldPlan (plain unit tests, no lifecycle); R3 reuses cmd/mindspec/adr_test.go's setupWorktreePair+chdir which fabricates the worktree/.git linkage on disk (no real git) and invokes adrShowCmd.RunE directly, plus a pure internal/adr parser test. Genuine RED pins confirmed for R1.AC1, R2.AC1, R3.AC1, R4.AC1, R4.AC2 (each fails against current source). The remaining ACs (R1.AC2/AC3 no-regression guards, R2.AC2 create-path guard, R3.AC2 parser lock) are legitimate non-vacuous guards, not tautologies, and the spec is explicit that R3.AC2 is a lock. NON-BLOCKING nits for the planner/author, not changes to the spec: (1) the Validation Proof '-run TestDivergence' matches ZERO existing tests (today's are TestValidateDivergence* / TestUnownedFileRejected); the new R1 tests must be named with a TestDivergence prefix or the proof should read TestValidateDivergence. Same for '-run TestCheckADRCoverage' (new) and '-run TestAdrShow' (new) -- all satisfiable by naming the new tests accordingly. (2) R3.AC1 references a 'root-resolution helper' that does not exist yet; invoking RunE satisfies it regardless. Neither nit prevents a hermetic RED pin, so APPROVE.
